title: March 5, 2026
summary: Added a fleet utilization dashboard with daily/weekly attainment rollups and live GPU fleet status telemetry.

features.title: Fleet Utilization Dashboard
features.summary: Track trailing 24h/7d and calendar daily/weekly GPU utilization attainment vs theoretical 100% capacity.
features.description: Added a new Fleet Utilization page at /fleet with real-time status plus historical rollups built from fleet telemetry checkins. The dashboard reports utilization attainment (achieved GPU-hours / possible GPU-hours), telemetry coverage, and per-machine breakdowns. It includes trailing windows (24h, 7d), daily buckets, weekly buckets, and current machine run state for operations monitoring.

features.title: Fleet Utilization API Endpoint
features.summary: New /fleet/utilization endpoint computes utilization rollups with conservative gap handling.
features.description: Implemented backend rollups from /home/don/apps/data/fleet/telemetry.jsonl. The API supports query parameters for day/week horizon, timezone, and carry-forward cap. Gap handling is conservative: stale samples only carry utilization credit for a bounded interval, preventing overstated utilization during telemetry silence.

features.title: Fleet API Auth Hardening
features.summary: Restricted anonymous fleet API access to checkin only; status/utilization now require app auth.
features.description: Tightened middleware authorization so only /api/fleet/checkin remains session-free for machine checkins using X-Fleet-Key. Fleet status and utilization reads now require authenticated user sessions and ma-options project access, reducing telemetry exposure while preserving collector functionality.

features.title: Order Sound Notifications
features.summary: Audible tones for entry fills, exit fills, and order rejections — no audio files needed.
features.description: Added procedurally-generated sound notifications using Web Audio API for key order events. Entry fills play an ascending two-note tone (C5→E5), exit fills play a descending tone (E5→C5), and rejections play a low buzz. Sounds hook into the existing 500ms account-events polling loop — zero additional network requests. A mute/unmute toggle on the Signals tab status bar persists to localStorage.
